title: Sarga 38
summary: Rama's divine birth, childhood, protection of Sage Vishwamitra's rituals from demons, breaking of Shiva's bow, and marriage to Sita. Addresses youth, childhood, education, overcoming early obstacles, and marriage.

sections.summary: Themes: self-knowledge, devotion, mind and meditation. The passage moves from “Thus, the mighty-armed Rama lived day after day, administering all affairs concerning both the city dwellers and the rural populace.” through “O King, I give all these jewels, which were collected for my sake, to my daughter.” through “Lakshmana will follow behind as a companion, taking abundant wealth and various jewels.”

Themes: self-knowledge, devotion, mind and meditation. The passage moves from “Yudhājit then said to Rama regarding his departure, "So be it; let the jewels and wealth indeed be inexhaustible in you alone."” through “Having said this much, the righteous Rama rose from his excellent seat and embraced him closely to his chest.” through “In that matter, I was merely an instrument; it was by your valor that Ravana, along with his retinue, sons, ministers, and relatives, was killed…”

Themes: self-knowledge, freedom from attachment. The passage moves from “You all were brought here by the great-souled Bharata, having heard that King Janaka's daughter was abducted from the forest.” through “Our greatest joy is that we see you, O Rama, victorious and with your enemies slain.” through “Honored by Rama, they all departed to their respective countries. इत्यार्षे श्रीमद्रामायणे वाल्मीकीये आदिकाव्ये श्रीमदुत्तरकाण्डे ऽष्टात्रिंशः…”

verses.sanskrit: एवमास्ते महाबाहुरहन्यहनि राघवः । प्रशासत्सर्वकार्याणि पौरजानपदेषु च ।। 7.38.1 ।।
verses.translation: Thus, the mighty-armed Rama lived day after day, administering all affairs concerning both the city dwellers and the rural populace.

verses.sanskrit: ततः कतिपयाहःसु वैदेहं मिथिलाधिपम् । राघवः प्राञ्जलिर्भूत्वा वाक्यमेतदुवाच ह ।। 7.38.2 ।।
verses.translation: Then, after a few days, Rama, with folded hands, spoke these words to Janaka, the king of Videha and lord of Mithila.

verses.sanskrit: भवान्हि गतिरव्यग्रा भवता पालिता वयम् । भवतस्तेजसोग्रेण रावणो निहतो मया ।। 7.38.3 ।।
verses.translation: You are indeed our steadfast refuge, and we have been protected by you; it was by your fierce might that Ravana was killed by me.

verses.sanskrit: इक्ष्वाकूणां च सर्वेषां मैथिलानां च सर्वशः । अतुलाः प्रीतयो राजन्सम्बन्धकपुरोगमाः ।। 7.38.4 ।।
verses.translation: O King, the affections between all the Ikshvakus and all the Maithilas, especially those stemming from our marital relationship, are truly incomparable.

verses.sanskrit: तद्भवान्स्वपुरं यातु रत्नान्यादाय पार्थिव । भरतश्च सहायार्थं पृष्ठतस्ते ऽनुयास्यति ।। 7.38.5 ।।
verses.translation: Therefore, O King, you may return to your city taking the jewels, and Bharata will follow behind you for assistance.

verses.sanskrit: स तथेति नृपः कृत्वा राघवं वाक्यमब्रवीत् । प्रीतो ऽस्मि भवतो राजन्दर्शनेन नयेन च ।। 7.38.6 ।।
verses.translation: Having agreed, King Janaka then spoke to Rama, saying, "O King, I am pleased by your sight and your wisdom."

verses.sanskrit: यान्येतानि तु रत्नानि मदर्थं सञ्चितानि वै । दुहित्रे तानि वै राजन्सर्वाण्येव ददामि च ।। 7.38.7 ।।
verses.translation: O King, I give all these jewels, which were collected for my sake, to my daughter.

verses.sanskrit: एवमुक्त्वा तु काकुत्स्थं जनको हृष्टमानसः । प्रययौ मिथिलां श्रीमांस्तमनुज्ञाय राघवम् ।। 7.38.8 ।।
verses.translation: Having thus spoken to Rama, the glorious Janaka, with a joyful mind, took leave of Rama and departed for Mithila.

verses.sanskrit: ततः प्रयाते जनके केकयं मातुलं प्रभुः । राघवः प्राञ्जलिर्भूत्वा वाक्यमेतदुवाच ह ।। 7.38.9 ।।
verses.translation: After Janaka had departed, Lord Rama, with folded hands, then spoke these words to his maternal uncle, Kekaya.

verses.sanskrit: इदं राज्यमहं चैव भरतश्च सलक्ष्मणः । आयत्तास्त्वं हि नो राजन्गतिश्च पुरुषर्षभ ।। 7.38.10 ।।
verses.translation: O King, O best among men, this kingdom, I, Bharata, and Lakshmana are all dependent on you, for you are indeed our refuge.

verses.sanskrit: राजा हि वृद्धः सन्तापं त्वदर्थमुपयास्यति । तस्माद्गमनमद्यैव रोचते तव पार्थिव ।। 7.38.11 ।।
verses.translation: The old king will indeed suffer sorrow for your sake, therefore, O prince, your departure today itself is suitable.

verses.sanskrit: लक्ष्मणेनानुयात्रेण पृष्ठतो ऽनुगमिष्यते । धनमादाय विपुलं रत्नानि विविधानि च ।। 7.38.12 ।।
verses.translation: Lakshmana will follow behind as a companion, taking abundant wealth and various jewels.

verses.sanskrit: युधाजित्तु तथेत्याह गमनं प्रति राघवम् । रत्नानि च धनं चैव त्वय्येवाक्षय्यमस्त्विति ।। 7.38.13 ।।
verses.translation: Yudhājit then said to Rama regarding his departure, "So be it; let the jewels and wealth indeed be inexhaustible in you alone."

verses.sanskrit: प्रदक्षिणं स राजानं कृत्वा केकयवर्धनः । रामेण हि कृतः पूर्वमभिवाद्य प्रदक्षिणम् ।। 7.38.14 ।।
verses.translation: Having circumambulated the king, Yudhājit, the enhancer of Kekaya, departed, just as Rama had previously saluted and circumambulated.

verses.sanskrit: लक्ष्मणेन सहायेन प्रयातः केकयेश्वरः । हते ऽसुरे यथा वृत्रे विष्णुना सह वासवः ।। 7.38.15 ।।
verses.translation: The lord of Kekaya departed with Lakshmana's help, just as Indra, with Vishnu, went after the demon Vritra was killed.

verses.sanskrit: तं विसृज्य ततो रामो वयस्यमकुतोभयम् । प्रतर्दनं काशिपतिं परिष्वज्येदमब्रवीत् ।। 7.38.16 ।।
verses.translation: Then, having dismissed that fearless friend, Rama embraced Pratardana, the lord of Kashi, and spoke this.

verses.sanskrit: दर्शिता भवता प्रीतिर्दर्शितं सौहृदं परम् । उद्योगश्च कृतो राजन्भरतेन त्वया सह ।। 7.38.17 ।।
verses.translation: O King, you have shown great affection and supreme friendship, and you, along with Bharata, have made a great effort.

verses.sanskrit: तद्भवानद्य काशेय पुरीं वाराणसीं व्रज । रमणीयां त्वया गुप्तां सुप्रकाशां सुतोरणाम् ।। 7.38.18 ।।
verses.translation: Therefore, O King of Kashi, you should go today to your beautiful city of Varanasi, which is protected by you, well-illuminated, and adorned with beautiful arches.

verses.sanskrit: एतावदुक्त्वा चोत्थाय काकुत्स्थः परमासनात् । पर्यष्वजत धर्मात्मा निरन्तरमुरोगतम् ।। 7.38.19 ।।
verses.translation: Having said this much, the righteous Rama rose from his excellent seat and embraced him closely to his chest.

verses.sanskrit: विसर्जयामास तदा कौसल्यानन्दवर्धनः । राघवेणाभ्यनुज्ञातः काशीशो ऽप्यकुतोभयः । वाराणसीं ययौ तूर्णं राघवेण विसर्जितः ।। 7.38.20 ।।
verses.translation: Then, Rama, who brings joy to Kaushalya, dismissed the fearless lord of Kashi, who, having been permitted and sent off by Rama, quickly returned to Varanasi.

verses.sanskrit: विसृज्य तं काशिपतिं त्रिशतं पृथिवीपतीन् । प्रहसन्राघवो वाक्यमुवाच मधुराक्षरम् ।। 7.38.21 ।।
verses.translation: Having dismissed that lord of Kashi and three hundred other kings, Rama, with a smile, spoke words that were sweet.

verses.sanskrit: भवतां प्रीतिरव्यग्रा तेजसा परिरक्षिता । धर्मश्च नियतो नित्यं सत्यं च भवतां सदा ।। 7.38.22 ।।
verses.translation: Your unwavering affection is protected by your valor, and your righteousness and truth are always constant.

verses.sanskrit: युष्माकं चानुभावेन तेजसा च महात्मनाम् । हतो दुरात्मा दुर्बुद्धी रावणो राक्षसाधमः ।। 7.38.23 ।।
verses.translation: It is by your influence and the valor of you great souls that the evil-minded, ill-intentioned Ravana, the vilest of rakshasas, was killed.

verses.sanskrit: हेतुमात्रमहं तत्र भवतां तेजसा हतः । रावणः सगणो युद्धे सपुत्रामात्यबान्धवः ।। 7.38.24 ।।
verses.translation: In that matter, I was merely an instrument; it was by your valor that Ravana, along with his retinue, sons, ministers, and relatives, was killed in battle.

verses.sanskrit: भवन्तश्च समानीता भरतेन महात्मना । श्रुत्वा जनकराजस्य काननात्तनयां हृताम् ।। 7.38.25 ।।
verses.translation: You all were brought here by the great-souled Bharata, having heard that King Janaka's daughter was abducted from the forest.

verses.sanskrit: उद्युक्तानां च सर्वेषां पार्थिवानां महात्मनाम् । कालो व्यतीतः सुमहान्गमनं रोचयाम्यतः ।। 7.38.26 ।।
verses.translation: A very long time has passed since all you great-souled kings were ready, therefore I approve of your departure.

verses.sanskrit: प्रत्यूचुस्तं च राजानो हर्षेण महता वृताः । दिष्ट्यां त्वं विजयी राम स्वराज्ये ऽपि प्रतिष्ठितः ।। 7.38.27 ।।
verses.translation: The kings, filled with great joy, replied to him, saying, "Fortunately, O Rama, you are victorious and re-established in your own kingdom."

verses.sanskrit: दिष्ट्या प्रत्याहृता सीता दिष्ट्या शत्रुः पराजितः । एष नः परमः काम एषा नः पीतिरुत्तमा ।। 7.38.28 ।।
verses.translation: Fortunately, Sita has been brought back, and fortunately, the enemy has been defeated; this is our highest desire and our supreme joy.

verses.sanskrit: यत्त्वां विजयिनं राम पश्यामो हतशात्रवम् ।। 7.38.29 ।।
verses.translation: Our greatest joy is that we see you, O Rama, victorious and with your enemies slain.

verses.sanskrit: एतत्त्वय्युपपन्नं च यदस्मांस्त्वं प्रशंससे । प्रशंसार्ह न जानीमः प्रशंसां वक्तुमीदृशीम् ।। 7.38.30 ।।
verses.translation: It is fitting for you to praise us, but O worthy of praise, we do not know how to utter such praise in return.

verses.sanskrit: आपृच्छामो गमिष्यामो हृदिस्थो नः सदा भवान् । वर्तामहे महाबाहो प्रीत्यात्र महता वृताः । भवेच्च ते महाराज प्रीतिरस्मासु नित्यदा ।। 7.38.31 ।।
verses.translation: We take our leave and shall depart, for you will always reside in our hearts; O mighty-armed one, we have been filled with great affection here, and O great king, may your affection for us always remain.

verses.sanskrit: बाढमित्येव राजानो हर्षेण परमान्विताः । उचुः प्राञ्जलयः सर्वे राघवं गमनोत्सुकाः ।। 7.38.32 ।।
verses.translation: All the kings, filled with supreme joy and eager to depart, said "Very well" to Rama with folded hands.

verses.sanskrit: पूजिताश्चैव रामेण जग्मुर्देशान्स्वकान्स्वकान् ।। 7.38.33 ।।
verses.translation: Honored by Rama, they all departed to their respective countries. इत्यार्षे श्रीमद्रामायणे वाल्मीकीये आदिकाव्ये श्रीमदुत्तरकाण्डे ऽष्टात्रिंशः सर्गः ।। 38 ।। Thus ends the thirty-eighth sarga of Uttara Kanda of the holy Ramayana the first epic composed by sage Valmiki.
